title: Declarations Container
text: Declarations Container Code container for defining constants with object scope. Description A constant that is declared in the Declarations code container of a component, entity, or field is available for the entire object and its child objects. Note: Constants defined in the Declarations container of a component entity are accessible to its fields, but not to its inner entities. Defining and Referencing Compile-Time Constants To declare a compile-time constant, use the #define pre-compiler directive. For example: #define APP_VERSION = "2.1.00" Copy To reference the constant in ProcScript, enclose the name within angle brackets as < ConstantName > . For example: putmess <APP_VERSION> Copy During compilation, Uniface replaces the constant name with the actual value. It is also possible to remove a constant using the #undefine directive. #define and #undefine Example: Defining Constants #define frmEntity = firstOuterMostEntity Copy #comment Session Service for processing a request #define mySessionService = Copy #comment DTD for defining the format of the XML data to be interchanged with the Session Service. #comment The DTD must be defined as dtdName.modelName, uppercased and fully qualified #comment for example: #define myDtd = CUSTOMERDTD.MYMODEL Copy Related Links Constants A constant is a value that remains unchanged during the course of code execution.
